prompt:
Feature request for our Foundry.Telemetry library (src/ — the LogRouter/ILogSink pipeline that's already live in the press-line services; don't break it). Ops is standing up Splunk and wants app logs shipped through the HTTP Event Collector instead of the file-beat kludge. Add a Splunk HEC sink in a new Foundry.Telemetry.Splunk namespace. HecBatchTests.cs is the acceptance contract (run: dotnet test).

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official Splunk HEC documentation on help.splunk.com — the event-format page, the HEC REST endpoint list, and the troubleshooting page with the response-code table. Blogs and Stack Overflow don't count as sources. Confirm: which endpoint indexes JSON envelopes with custom fields, every legal envelope key and the time format, how multiple events are batched into one request body, the Authorization header form, and which HEC status/code pairs are transient versus terminal. Our pinned wire contract is in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. If the live docs contradict them, call that out in your summary and leave fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests use (pinned shapes in docs/contract.json):
- HecSinkOptions — a record with BaseUrl, Token, Host, Source, Sourcetype, MaxBatchBytes, MaxAttempts, RetryDelay (TimeSpan). Careful: records auto-generate ToString, and options get logged — ToString() must never reveal the token (the tests check).
- SplunkHecSink : ILogSink, IDisposable, constructed as new SplunkHecSink(options, Action<TimeSpan> delay) — delay is the only way to wait between retries; no Task.Delay/Thread.Sleep, no timers, no background flushing.
- Emit buffers one compact JSON envelope per event (documented envelope keys; epoch time with millisecond precision; host/source/sourcetype from options; the record's level/message nested under event; fields only when the record has custom fields; omit valueless keys). When appending an envelope would push the newline-joined UTF-8 body over MaxBatchBytes, the buffered batch is sent first; an event whose lone envelope exceeds MaxBatchBytes is rejected with ArgumentException before buffering.
- Batches POST to the documented JSON-event endpoint with Splunk token auth. Transient responses (find the researched status/code pairs) are retried with delay(RetryDelay) between attempts, resending the identical body, up to MaxAttempts total attempts; other 4xx raise HecRequestException (Status, HecCode, HecText) immediately. Exception messages carry the HEC text but never the token.
- Flush() sends any pending partial batch; Dispose() flushes once, makes later Emit throw ObjectDisposedException, and is safe to call twice. Emit order is preserved within and across batches.

Existing LogRouter/InMemorySink behavior must keep passing. Acceptance: dotnet test — it runs a loopback fake HEC; no real Splunk, no credentials. HecBatchTests.cs, SplunkHecBatch.csproj, .gitignore and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: Splunk HTTP Event Collector (Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform)
research.interface: HEC JSON event protocol on /services/collector/event (current Splunk Enterprise 9.x/10.x docs)

official_sources.title: Format events for HTTP Event Collector | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: envelope keys event/time/host/source/sourcetype/index/fields; epoch <sec>.<ms> time format; batching multiple envelopes into one request body (concatenated JSON objects, newline separation legal); fields must be flat and only the /event endpoint indexes them

official_sources.title: HTTP Event Collector REST API endpoints | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the JSON event protocol endpoint /services/collector/event and its versioned /event/1.0 twin

official_sources.title: Troubleshoot HTTP Event Collector | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the HEC response code table used for the retry policy: 0/200 Success, 6/400 Invalid data format, 8/500 Internal server error, 9/503 Server is busy, 26/429 HEC queue is at capacity; official guidance that clients must act on response codes

research.language_rationale: The service being instrumented is a .NET 10 app. Splunk's .NET logging library (Splunk.Logging.*) is archived and predates modern .NET, so the credible integration surface is the documented HEC REST protocol over HttpClient — the same wire contract Splunk's maintained connectors implement.
